---
title: "Publications"
body-classes: "publications-page"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-location: right          # Right margin (default, but explicit)
    toc-title: "On this page"    # Matches your screenshot/circle
    toc-depth: 2                 # Shows ## headings (major sections)
    toc-expand: true             # Fully expanded by default for quick scan
    css: styles.css              # Keep if you have custom styles
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/font-awesome/6.5.0/css/all.min.css">
---
<div class="pub-stats">
  <div class="pub-stat"><span class="pub-stat-number">6</span><span class="pub-stat-label">Published</span></div>
  <div class="pub-stat"><span class="pub-stat-number">9</span><span class="pub-stat-label">Under Review</span></div>
  <div class="pub-stat"><span class="pub-stat-number">3</span><span class="pub-stat-label">Conference Papers</span></div>
  <div class="pub-stat"><span class="pub-stat-number">2</span><span class="pub-stat-label">Presentations</span></div>
</div>
Here you will find my peer-reviewed journal articles, manuscripts under review, conference proceedings, and research presentations. My work focuses on veterinary epidemiology, infectious disease modelling, Bayesian prevalence estimation, risk factor analysis, and production-impact assessment in dairy cattle and other ruminant populations in Bangladesh.

My research applies Bayesian latent-class models, hierarchical and mixed-effects regression, multinomial disease-state modelling, mixture models, and applied epidemiological statistics to investigate zoonotic and production-limiting diseases, including brucellosis, paratuberculosis, bovine viral diarrhoea, *Coxiella burnetii*, subclinical mastitis, and fascioliasis.

I have contributed as first author, equally contributing first author, or major analytical contributor across several studies, with roles spanning study design, field sampling, laboratory diagnostics, data curation, statistical analysis, Bayesian modelling, visualisation, and manuscript preparation.

## Published Journal Articles {.published}

<i class="fa-solid fa-check-circle" style="color: #28a745;"></i> **Published**

- Kibria, A.M., **Islam, M.N.**, Alam, M.S., Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Rafeh, A.A., Hasan, R.S.M., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Estimated true prevalence and associated risk factors of bovine paratuberculosis antibodies in dairy herds in Bangladesh. *PLOS ONE*.  
  [https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0334683](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0334683)  
  **Equally contributing first author** — Study design, methodology development, Bayesian latent-class prevalence estimation, data analysis, visualization, and manuscript preparation.

- Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Mahmud, R.S., Rahman, M.S., Ariful Islam, M., Aktaruzzaman, M., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Risk factors for latent and active brucellosis in dairy cattle: a Bayesian mixed-effects multinomial regression analysis in large-scale Bangladeshi herds. *Animal Diseases*, 6, 26.  
  [https://doi.org/10.1186/s44149-026-00234-w](https://doi.org/10.1186/s44149-026-00234-w)  
  **Major analytical and manuscript contributor** — Sample collection and laboratory analysis, data curation, statistical analyses, Bayesian mixed-effects multinomial modelling, and manuscript drafting.

- Mahmud Sajeeb, M.S., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Islam, M.M., Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Prevalence and risk factors of bovine viral diarrhea virus antibodies in dairy herds of Bangladesh. *Veterinary Sciences*, 12(8), 739.  
  [https://doi.org/10.3390/vetsci12080739](https://doi.org/10.3390/vetsci12080739)  
  **Equally contributing first author** — Study design, field sampling, ELISA diagnostics, multivariable regression analysis, visualization, and manuscript preparation.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., Ariful Islam, M., Shanta, S.A., Tahomina Akter, M., Hossain, M.T., **Islam, M.N.**, Alam, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Prevalence and cow-level risk factors for mild and severe subclinical mastitis in intensive and extensive dairy systems in Bangladesh. *Veterinary Research Communications*, 50, 447.  
  [https://doi.org/10.1007/s11259-026-11387-x](https://doi.org/10.1007/s11259-026-11387-x)  
  **Contributor** — Fieldwork, data curation, and laboratory analysis.

- Abu Saif, M., **Islam, M.N.**, Rahman, A.K.M.A., Ferdous, M.J., Islam, R., & Bari, F.Y. (2026).  
  Integrated immune and protein profiles predict growth in preweaning lambs independent of feeding regimen and maternal oxytocin administration. *Iraqi Journal of Veterinary Sciences*, 40(2), 397–403.  
  [https://doi.org/10.33899/ijvs.2026.168586.4671](https://doi.org/10.33899/ijvs.2026.168586.4671)  
  **Contributor** — Study design, methodology development, and manuscript preparation.

- Abu Saif, M., **Islam, M.N.**, Ferdous, M.J., Rahman, A.K.M.A., & Bari, F.Y. (2026).  
  Early-life feeding and management interventions on lamb survivability. *Bulgarian Journal of Veterinary Medicine* (online first).  
  [https://doi.org/10.15547/bjvm.2026-0050](https://doi.org/10.15547/bjvm.2026-0050)  
  **Contributor** — Study design, methodology development, formal analysis, and manuscript preparation.
  
## Manuscripts Under Review {.under-review}

<i class="fa-solid fa-hourglass-half" style="color: #fd7e14;"></i> **Under Review**

### First or Equally Contributing First Author

- **Islam, M.N.**, Ferdous, M.J., Ema, A.A., Haque, M.Z., Alam, M.S., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Contrasting age-related seroepidemiological signatures of Q fever and neosporosis in large-scale commercial dairy herds in Bangladesh: a paired hierarchical Bayesian catalytic analysis. *Preventive Veterinary Medicine*. *(First author)*

- **Islam, M.N.**, Haque, M.Z., Alam, M.S., Islam, S., Adhikari, B.J., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Bayesian estimation of the true prevalence of *Coxiella burnetii* milk antibodies and associated risk factors in dairy herds in Bangladesh. *Veterinary Medicine and Science*. *(First author)*

- Ferdous, M.J., **Islam, M.N.**, Alam, M.S., Saif, M.A., Rahman, M.A., Aktaruzzaman, M., Rahman, M.S., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Age-stratified transmission heterogeneity of bovine brucellosis in large commercial dairy herds in Bangladesh: a hierarchical Bayesian catalytic analysis. *Epidemics*. *(Equally contributing first author)*

- Saha, U.K., **Islam, M.N.**, Chaudhary, P., Hassan Alif, M.K., Mim, S.N., & Rahman, M.S. (2026).  
  Exploratory longitudinal ELISA antibody responses and hematological parameters after heat-inactivated *Brucella abortus* vaccination in cattle. *Bulgarian Journal of Veterinary Medicine*. *(Equally contributing first author)*

### Co-Author

- Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Rahman, M.S., Islam, M.A., Aktaruzzaman, M., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Economic burden and cost-effectiveness of brucellosis control strategies in large commercial dairy herds in Bangladesh: a Bayesian decision-analytic study. *Preventive Veterinary Medicine*.

- Anwarul Islam, **Islam, M.N.**, Ferdous, M.J., Alam, M.S., Rahman, M.A., Mahmud, R.S., Alam, M.M., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Economic burden and strategic control of ruminant fascioliasis in Bangladesh. *Journal of Helminthology*.

- Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Adhikari, B.J., Islam, S., Mahmud, R.S., Rahman, M.S., Ariful Islam, M., Aktaruzzaman, M., Meletis, L., Kostoulas, P., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2026).  
  Bayesian hierarchical mixture modelling to derive probabilistic iELISA thresholds for bovine brucellosis in Bangladeshi dairy herds. *PLOS ONE*.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., Ariful Islam, M., Shanta, S.A., Tahomina Akter, M., Hossain, M.T., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, Meletis, L., Kostoulas, P., & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Bayesian Gaussian mixture modelling to determine somatic cell count thresholds for subclinical mastitis diagnosis in dairy cows. *The Journal of Veterinary Medical Science*.

- Aktaruzzaman, M., Tahomina Akter, M., Mazhar Islam, M., Ariful Islam, M., Hossain, M.T., Alam, M.S., **Islam, M.N.**, & Rahman, A.K.M.A. (2025).  
  Economic impact of subclinical mastitis in Bangladeshi crossbred dairy cattle. *Preventive Veterinary Medicine*.

## Conference Proceedings & Papers

- **Islam, M.**; Alam, M.; Islam, S.; Adhikari, B.; Rahman, A. (2025).  
  Feasibility of on-farm somatic cell count screening for subclinical mastitis: Prevalence and risk factors in a commercial dairy herd in Bangladesh. In *Proceedings of the 1st International Online Conference on Veterinary Sciences*, 3–5 December 2025, MDPI: Basel, Switzerland.  
  [https://sciforum.net/paper/view/27422](https://sciforum.net/paper/view/27422)

- Alam, M. S., **Islam, M. N.**, Rafeh, A. A., Adhikari, B. J., Kibria, M. A., Mahmud, M. S., Ema, A. A., Islam, S., Rahman, M. S., Rahman, A. K. M. A. (2025).  
  Herd-level prevalence of brucellosis, paratuberculosis, neosporosis, and bovine viral diarrhea in large dairy herds in Bangladesh. *BAU Research Progress*, 35.

- Alam, M. S., **Islam, M. N.**, Rafeh, A. A., Rahman, M. S., Islam, M. A., & Rahman, A. K. M. (2024).  
  Herd-level prevalence of brucellosis in large dairy herds in Bangladesh. *Proceedings of the BSVER Annual Scientific Conference 30*, Bangladesh Agricultural University, Mymensingh, February 24-25, 2024.

## Conference Presentations & Posters

- **Feasibility of On-Farm Somatic Cell Count Screening for Subclinical Mastitis: Prevalence and Risk Factors in a Commercial Dairy Herd in Bangladesh.**  
  Presented at IOCVS 2025, 4 December 2025. [DOI: 10.13140/RG.2.2.16208.16642](https://doi.org/10.13140/RG.2.2.16208.16642).

- **Prevalence and Epidemiology of Bovine Brucellosis in Large Dairy Farms of Bangladesh.**  
  Research poster on prevalence, risk factors, and control strategies presented at the BSVER Annual Scientific Conference 2024, Bangladesh Agricultural University, Mymensingh, February 24-25, 2024.
